nodes.label: Unit 1: Wake Up! / Neha's Alarm Clock
nodes.description: A poem about waking up early and a story about a girl who learns punctuality

nodes.label: Wake Up! (Poem)
nodes.description: A cheerful poem that encourages children to wake up early and enjoy nature

nodes.label: Nature in the Morning
nodes.description: Birds singing, flowers blooming, sun shining, breeze blowing

nodes.label: Message of the Poem
nodes.description: Wake up early, do not be lazy, enjoy the beauty of the morning

nodes.label: Rhyming Words
nodes.description: The poem uses rhyming words to make it fun and musical to read aloud

nodes.label: Neha's Alarm Clock (Story)
nodes.description: A story about a girl named Neha who learns to wake up on time

nodes.label: Neha's Problem
nodes.description: Neha cannot wake up early and is always late for school

nodes.label: The Alarm Clock Solution
nodes.description: An alarm clock helps Neha wake up on time every morning

nodes.label: Lesson Learned
nodes.description: Punctuality, discipline, and taking responsibility for your habits

nodes.label: Grammar: Sentences
nodes.description: Understanding what makes a complete sentence

nodes.label: Parts of a Sentence
nodes.description: Subject (who or what) and Predicate (what happens)

nodes.label: Sentence Rules
nodes.description: Start with a capital letter, end with full stop or question mark or exclamation mark

nodes.label: Sentence vs. Phrase
nodes.description: A sentence makes complete sense; a phrase does not

nodes.label: Connecting Theme
nodes.description: Both the poem and the story share the theme of waking up early and good morning habits
